seed_problem: Select a maximum-weight set of mutually non-conflicting research sites on a general conflict graph; checker validates independence and scores weight/baseline.
mutation: input: relax the conflict structure from separable clusters to a general graph -- dense household/workplace cliques wired together by sparse cross-cluster contacts plus a few high-weight high-degree super-connector vertices -- so max-weight independent set is NP-hard and weight-greedy, weight/degree-greedy, and randomized local search diverge.
title: Quiet Cohort: Sentinel Sampling on a Pandemic Contact Net
strategies: single-best baseline: monitor only the highest-value individual (always independent, scores the calibration point)
weight-greedy: consider individuals in descending surveillance value and add each one whose contacts are all still unmonitored
weight-over-degree greedy: order by value/(contacts+1) so cheap-to-block low-degree individuals are grabbed before high-degree super-connectors
randomized multi-restart local search: run many perturbed greedy orderings and keep the maximal independent set of highest total value
